- Traffic profile: steady-state ~300 QPS, peak bursts to ~1k QPS; streaming sessions (long-lived) expected concurrency ~40–60 during peak.
- Payload: average prompt ~650 token...

text:
From: Jamie Park <jamie@scalecart.com>
To: ava.chen@redwood.ai
Cc: sre@scalecart.com
Date: 2027-02-20T08:12:00-08:00
Subject: Production: spike in 5xx/429 immediately after gamma-v2-quant rollout

Ava —

We just pushed your gamma-v2-quant variant at 07:45 UTC for our product recommendations endpoint (canary -> full rollout per instructions). Within ~10–30 minutes we saw an error amplification: 5xx rate jumped from ~2.4% to 12.7% and 429s from ~0.1% to 9.3%. Latency tails spiked and clients started retrying aggressively, which made the issue worse.

We're cutting a handful of numbers from our monitoring:
- Peak 5xx window (07:58–08:05 UTC): 15%
- 429 count increased 90x relative to baseline in 20m
- P95 latency went from 180ms -> 1.1s

Attached are a trimmed packet of logs and a chart (support_logs_20270220.zip, error_rate_surge_chart.png). Our SRE (cc) is standing by. Please advise ne...

2) We typically require either (a) a fixed monthly invoice amount tied to an annual commitment, or (b) a usage-based PO where overages are billed per unit but must have a hard cap or a pre-approved escalation path. The vendor form as-is does...

Date: Mon, 8 Feb 2027 16:03:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call: Technical fit & buying criteria for EpiHealth LLM exit

Hi Sam — thanks again for taking time today. Quick written recap from our call so we have alignment and clear next steps.

Key pain points you shared:
- Vendor lock-in with current proprietary APIs (OpenAI + Anthropic) — need a migration path that preserves behavior and minimizes engineering lift.
- Cost unpredictability at scale (embedding + chat token spend).
- Latency spikes and inconsistent 95th pct performance for interactive clinical workflows.
- Data governance: PHI handling, audit trail, and KMS integration.

Current stack (what we heard):
- Product: conversational clinical assistant + semantic search for notes.

Cc: ops@brightcart-internal@example.com
Date: Tue, 22 Dec 2026 09:06:00 -0800
Subject: Intermittent P99 bifurcation impacting Checkoutly - urgent ops sync

Team —

We started seeing a split in the p99 distribution on /v1/generate-checkout (Checkoutly tenant) at ~08:50 PST. Two clear modes: a ~220ms mode and a tailing mode hitting 3.2s for ~1.8% of requests over the last 15m. Throughput is nominal (QPS steady), but the high tail correlates with large context-length requests from a small subset of users (>1k tokens). Attached is the p99_bifurcation_graph.png and a small sample of logs (checkpoint_sample_logs.zip).

Immediate asks:
- SRE (Soojin / Karthik): can we triage whether this is kernel scheduling (multi-tenant eviction) vs KV-cache warmup?...
